compiled_state.title: test-bridge-observability-e2e.sh fails in batch runner due to temp dir reuse across SIGURG restarts
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Observed Behavior
test-bridge-observability-e2e.sh fails with 'No such file or directory' for /var/folders/.../tmp.XXXXX/repo when run inside test-batched.sh across multiple SIGURG-interrupted runs.

## Expected Behavior
Test passes when run standalone: bash tests/scripts/test-bridge-observability-e2e.sh exits 0.

## Root Cause
The test creates temp directories that later runs expect to still exist. When test-batched.sh restarts after SIGURG, it resumes mid-sequence and the temp dirs from step 11 scaffolding are gone.

## Impact
6 tests fail in batched mode: step11, step11b, scenario_bridge_observability_and_recovery_e2e. All pass standalone.

comments.body: Investigation: test passes standalone (bash tests/scripts/test-bridge-observability-e2e.sh exits 0). Failure only occurs in test-batched.sh with multiple SIGURG interruptions. Root cause is likely that test-batched.sh exits on SIGURG without killing the subprocess (CMD_PID), causing the subprocess to complete and clean up its temp dirs. On resume, test-batched.sh re-runs the test. If macOS or the previous run's EXIT trap cleaned up the temp dirs between runs, the re-run creates fresh dirs and should succeed. The exact failure mode requires empirical reproduction with controlled SIGURG events — not reproducible analytically.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Escalation: failure is environment-dependent (only occurs with multiple SIGURG interruptions in test-batched.sh; passes standalone). Root cause not reproducible analytically. The test creates fresh temp dirs via mktemp -d each run (no state reuse). Requires empirical reproduction with controlled SIGURG injection to diagnose further. Escalating to user for decision on how to proceed.
comments.author: Test
